steps.title: Identify the sentence parts
steps.content: The phrase beginning with "With over three millennia" is an introductory modifier. The main clause begins with "a single tree like this," so the modifier needs a comma before that subject.

steps.title: Punctuate the modifier
steps.content: A makes the introductory modifier a fragment, B uses a semicolon after a non-clause, and C incorrectly joins the modifier to the subject with "and." The comma after "rings" and the article "a" correctly introduce the main clause. The answer is D.
